main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Macros


#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'
#define VERSION ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL
#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Typedefs


typedef int(* mode_entry_point_t) (void)

Functions


atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit)
bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)
int main (int argc, char *argv[])

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

This file implements the main entry point for the unified ascii-chat binary, which provides server, client, mirror, and discovery service functionality in a single executable.

The dispatcher now delegates ALL option parsing (including mode detection and binary-level options) to options_init(), then simply dispatches to the appropriate mode-specific entry point based on the detected mode.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

3.0

Definition in file main.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'

#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \


  if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \


    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \


    _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Definition at line 181 of file main.c.


181 #define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()                                                                                      \
182   do {                                                                                                                 \
183     static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \
184     if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \
185       NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \
186       _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \
187     }                                                                                                                  \
188   } while (0)

atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO

Global flag indicating application should exit (used by all

References g_should_exit.






int
  main (int argc, char * argv[])


Definition at line 463 of file main.c.


463                                  {
464   // Set global argc/argv for early argv inspection (e.g., in terminal.c)
465   g_argc = argc;
466   g_argv = argv;
467 
468   // Validate basic argument structure
469   if (argc < 1 || argv == NULL || argv[0] == NULL) {
470     return 1;
471   }
472 
473   // Show cursor early in case a previous session crashed with it hidden
474   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
475 
476   // Initialize the named registry for debugging (allows --debug-state to show registered synchronization primitives)
477 #ifndef NDEBUG
478   named_init();
479   // Initialize atomic operations debug tracking
480   debug_atomic_init();
481   // Register global atomics with descriptive names for debug sync state monitoring
482   REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS();
483   // Static atomics are now auto-registered via ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO macro at definition time
484   // Register all packet types from the packet_type_t enum
485   named_registry_register_packet_types();
486 #endif
487 
488   // Register the main thread IMMEDIATELY after named_init() to ensure it's available for all subsequent allocations
489 #ifndef NDEBUG
490   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD(asciichat_thread_self(), "main", NULL);
491   // Also save main thread ID for memory reporting (must be very early)
492   debug_sync_set_main_thread_id();
493 #endif
494 
495   // VERY FIRST: Scan for --color BEFORE ANY logging initialization
496   // This sets global flags that persist through cleanup, enabling --color to force colors
497   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
498     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--color=true") == 0) {
499       g_color_flag_passed = true;
500       g_color_flag_value = true;
501       break;
502     } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color=false") == 0) {
503       g_color_flag_passed = true;
504       g_color_flag_value = false;
505       break;
506     } else if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
507       g_color_flag_passed = true;
508       // Default to true for any other --color=X value
509       g_color_flag_value = true;
510       break;
511     }
512   }
513 
514   // SECOND: Scan for --grep BEFORE ANY logging initialization
515   // This ensures ALL logs (including from shared_init) can be filtered
516   // Supports multiple --grep patterns (ORed together)
517   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
518     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--grep") == 0) {
519       const char *pattern = argv[i + 1];
520       asciichat_error_t filter_result = grep_init(pattern);
521       if (filter_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
522         log_error("Invalid --grep pattern or invalid flags: \"%s\" - use /pattern/flags format (e.g., \"/query/ig\" or \"/literal/F\")", pattern);
523         return 1;
524       }
525       i++; // Skip the pattern argument
526     }
527   }
528 
529   // Detect terminal capabilities early so colored help output works
530   // Logging will be initialized by asciichat_shared_init() before options_init()
531   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
532   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
533   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
534 
535   // Warn if Release build was built from dirty working tree
536 #if ASCII_CHAT_GIT_IS_DIRTY
537   if (strcmp(ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, "Release") == 0) {
538     // Dirty git warning will be logged after logging initialization
539   }
540 #endif
541 
542   // Load color scheme early (from config files and CLI) before logging initialization
543   // This allows logging to use the correct colors from the start
544   options_colorscheme_init_early(argc, argv);
545 
546   // Parse --color-scheme from argv early to set logging colors for help output
547   const char *colorscheme_name = "pastel"; // default
548   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
549     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color-scheme") == 0) {
550       colorscheme_name = argv[i + 1];
551       break;
552     }
553   }
554 
555   // Load and apply colorscheme to logging BEFORE options_init() so help gets colors
556   color_scheme_t scheme;
557   if (colorscheme_load_builtin(colorscheme_name, &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
558     log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
559   } else {
560     // Fall back to default pastel if scheme not found
561     if (colorscheme_load_builtin("pastel", &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
562       log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
563     }
564   }
565 
566   // Initialize logging colors so they're ready for help output
567   log_init_colors();
568 
569   // EARLY PARSE: Find the mode position (first positional argument)
570   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
571   int mode_position = -1;
572   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
573     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
574       // Found a positional argument - this is the mode or session string
575       mode_position = i;
576       break;
577     }
578 
579     // Skip option and its argument if needed
580     // Check if this is an option that takes a required argument
581     const char *arg = argv[i];
582     const char *opt_name = arg;
583     if (arg[0] == '-') {
584       opt_name = arg + (arg[1] == '-' ? 2 : 1);
585     }
586 
587     // Handle --option=value format
588     if (strchr(opt_name, '=')) {
589       continue; // Value is part of this arg, no need to skip next
590     }
591 
592     // Options that take required arguments
593     if (strcmp(arg, "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "-L") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-level") == 0 ||
594         strcmp(arg, "--config") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--color-scheme") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-template") == 0) {
595       if (i + 1 < argc) {
596         i++; // Skip the argument value
597       }
598     }
599   }
600 
601   // EARLY PARSE: Determine mode from argv to know if this is client-like mode
602   // The first positional argument (after options) is the mode: client, server, mirror, discovery, acds
603   bool is_client_like_mode = false;
604   if (mode_position > 0) {
605     const char *first_arg = argv[mode_position];
606     if (strcmp(first_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "mirror") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "discovery") == 0) {
607       is_client_like_mode = true;
608     }
609   }
610 
611   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log file from argv (--log-file or -L)
612   // Must appear BEFORE the mode
613   // Generate default log path based on build type and mode
614   char default_log_path[PATH_MAX];
615   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_INVALID;
616   if (mode_position > 0) {
617     const char *mode_str = argv[mode_position];
618     // Convert mode string to enum
619     if (strcmp(mode_str, "server") == 0) {
620       detected_mode = MODE_SERVER;
621     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "client") == 0) {
622       detected_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
623     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "mirror") == 0) {
624       detected_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
625     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "acds") == 0 || strcmp(mode_str, "discovery-service") == 0) {
626       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
627     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "discovery") == 0) {
628       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
629     }
630   }
631   generate_default_log_path(detected_mode, default_log_path, sizeof(default_log_path));
632 
633   const char *log_file = default_log_path;
634   int max_search = (mode_position > 0) ? mode_position : argc;
635   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
636     if ((strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0)) {
637       log_file = argv[i + 1];
638       break;
639     }
640   }
641 
642   // EARLY PARSE: Check for --json (JSON logging format)
643   // If JSON format is enabled, we'll use the JSON filename during early init
644   // --json MUST appear BEFORE the mode
645   bool early_json_format = false;
646   if (mode_position > 0) {
647     for (int i = 1; i < mode_position; i++) {
648       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
649         early_json_format = true;
650         break;
651       }
652     }
653   } else {
654     // If no mode found, search entire argv
655     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
656       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
657         early_json_format = true;
658         break;
659       }
660     }
661   }
662 
663   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log template from argv (--log-template)
664   // Note: This is for format templates, different from --log-format which is the output format
665   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
666   const char *early_log_template = NULL;
667   bool early_log_template_console_only = false;
668   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
669     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-template") == 0) {
670       early_log_template = argv[i + 1];
671       break;
672     }
673   }
674   for (int i = 1; i < max_search; i++) {
675     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-format-console-only") == 0) {
676       early_log_template_console_only = true;
677       break;
678     }
679   }
680 
681   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
682   // This ensures options parsing can use properly configured logging with colors
683   // If JSON format is requested, don't write text logs to file
684   // All logs will be JSON formatted later once options_init() runs
685   const char *early_log_file = early_json_format ? NULL : log_file;
686   asciichat_error_t init_result = asciichat_shared_init(early_log_file, is_client_like_mode);
687   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
688     return init_result;
689   }
690 
691   // Route logs to stderr if stdout is piped (MUST happen early, before options_init logs)
692   // This keeps stdout clean for data output (e.g., --snapshot mode piped to file)
693   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
694     log_set_force_stderr(true);
695   }
696 
697   // Register cleanup of shared subsystems to run on normal exit
698   // Library code doesn't call atexit() - the application is responsible
699   // IMPORTANT: atexit handlers run in LIFO order - last registered runs first
700   // So register order (top to bottom) = execution order (bottom to top):
701   // 1. PCRE2 cleanup (runs last) - frees compiled regexes after all code stops using them
702   // 2. Path cleanup (runs 3rd) - now a no-op but kept for API compatibility
703   // 3. Shared destroy (runs 2nd) - frees all shared resources
704   // 4. Cursor show (runs 1st) - shows cursor before exit
705   (void)atexit(asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all);
706   (void)atexit(path_cleanup_thread_locals);
707   (void)atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy);
708   (void)atexit(on_exit_show_cursor);
709 
710   // SECRET: Check for --backtrace (debug builds only) BEFORE options_init()
711   // Prints a backtrace and exits immediately - useful for debugging hangs
712 #ifndef NDEBUG
713   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
714     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
715       log_info("=== Backtrace at startup ===");
716       backtrace_print_simple(0);
717       log_info("=== End Backtrace ===");
718       asciichat_shared_destroy();
719       return 0;
720     }
721   }
722 #endif
723 
724   // NOW parse all options - can use logging with colors!
725   asciichat_error_t options_result = options_init(argc, argv);
726   if (options_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
727     asciichat_error_context_t error_ctx;
728     if (HAS_ERRNO(&error_ctx)) {
729       log_error("Error: %s", error_ctx.context_message);
730     } else {
731       log_error("Error: Failed to initialize options (error code: %d)", options_result);
732     }
733 
734     // Clean up options state before exiting
735     options_state_destroy();
736     options_cleanup_schema();
737 
738     exit(options_result);
739   }
740 
741   // Get parsed options
742   const options_t *opts = options_get();
743   if (!opts) {
744     log_error("Error: Options not initialized");
745     return 1;
746   }
747 
748   // Determine final log file path (use mode-specific default from options if available)
749   // Determine output format early to decide on filename and logging strategy
750   bool use_json_logging = GET_OPTION(json);
751 
752   // Determine the log filename
753   // Check if user explicitly passed --log-file (not just the mode-specific default from options_init)
754   bool user_specified_log_file = false;
755   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
756     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0) {
757       user_specified_log_file = true;
758       break;
759     }
760   }
761 
762   const char *final_log_file = opts->log_file;
763   char json_filename_buf[256];
764 
765   if (use_json_logging) {
766     // When JSON logging is enabled, determine JSON output filename
767     if (user_specified_log_file) {
768       // User explicitly specified a log file - use it exactly for JSON output
769       SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
770     } else {
771       // Using default: replace .log with .json in the mode-specific default
772       // e.g., server.log -> server.json, mirror.log -> mirror.json, etc.
773       size_t len = strlen(final_log_file);
774       if (len > 4 && strcmp(&final_log_file[len - 4], ".log") == 0) {
775         // File ends with .log - replace with .json
776         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
777         // Replace .log with .json
778         strcpy(&json_filename_buf[len - 4], ".json");
779       } else {
780         // File doesn't end with .log - just append .json
781         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
782         strncat(json_filename_buf, ".json", sizeof(json_filename_buf) - strlen(json_filename_buf) - 1);
783       }
784     }
785     final_log_file = json_filename_buf;
786     // For JSON mode: Initialize logging without file (text will be disabled)
787     // We'll set up JSON output separately below
788     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
789   } else {
790     // Text logging mode: use the file from options (which is mode-specific default or user-specified)
791     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
792   }
793 
794   // Apply custom log template if specified (use early parsed value if available, otherwise use options)
795   const char *final_format = early_log_template ? early_log_template : GET_OPTION(log_template);
796   bool final_format_console_only =
797       early_log_template ? early_log_template_console_only : GET_OPTION(log_format_console_only);
798   if (final_format && final_format[0] != '\0') {
799     asciichat_error_t fmt_result = log_set_format(final_format, final_format_console_only);
800     if (fmt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
801       log_error("Failed to apply custom log format");
802     }
803   }
804 
805   // Configure JSON output if requested
806   if (use_json_logging) {
807     // Open the JSON file for output
808     int json_fd = platform_open("main_log_file", final_log_file, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
809     if (json_fd >= 0) {
810       log_set_json_output(json_fd);
811     } else {
812       // Failed to open JSON file - report error and exit
813       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open JSON output file: %s", final_log_file);
814       return ERROR_CONFIG;
815     }
816   }
817 
818   // Initialize colors now that logging is fully initialized
819   // This must happen after log_init() since log_init_colors() checks if g_log.initialized
820   log_init_colors();
821 
822   // Apply quiet mode - disables terminal output
823   // Status screen mode only disables terminal output if terminal is interactive
824   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
825   if (GET_OPTION(quiet) ||
826       (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive())) {
827     log_set_terminal_output(false);
828   }
829 
830   // Initialize palette based on command line options
831   const char *custom_chars = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : NULL;
832   if (apply_palette_config(GET_OPTION(palette_type), custom_chars) != 0) {
833     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to apply palette configuration");
834   }
835 
836   // Set quiet mode for memory debugging
837 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
838   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(GET_OPTION(quiet));
839 #endif
840 
841   // Truncate log if it's already too large
842   log_truncate_if_large();
843   fflush(stderr);
844 
845   // Handle --help and --version (these are detected and flagged by options_init)
846   // Terminal capabilities already initialized before options_init() at startup
847   if (opts->help) {
848     print_usage(opts->detected_mode);
849     fflush(NULL);
850     _Exit(0);
851   }
852 
853   if (opts->version) {
854     log_set_terminal_output(true);
855     action_show_version();
856     // action_show_version() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
857   }
858 
859 #ifndef NDEBUG
860   // Handle --debug-state (debug builds only)
861   // Sleep for specified time AFTER mode initialization so locks are created
862   if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT(debug_sync_state_time, opts) && opts->debug_sync_state_time > 0.0) {
863     log_info("Will print sync state after %f seconds", opts->debug_sync_state_time);
864   }
865 #endif
866 
867   // For server mode with status screen: disable terminal output only if interactive
868   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
869   // The status screen (when shown) will capture and display logs in its buffer instead
870   if (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && opts->status_screen && terminal_is_interactive()) {
871     log_set_terminal_output(false);
872   }
873 
874   log_dev("Logging initialized to %s", final_log_file);
875 
876   // Note: We do NOT auto-disable colors when stdout appears to be piped, because:
877   // 1. Tools like ripgrep can display ANSI colors when piped
878   // 2. Sandboxed/containerized environments may report false positives for isatty()
879   // 3. Users can explicitly disable colors with --color=false if needed
880   // Color behavior is now fully controlled by --color and --color-mode options.
881 
882 #ifndef NDEBUG
883   // Initialize lock debugging system after logging is fully set up
884   log_debug("Initializing lock debug system...");
885   int debug_sync_result = debug_sync_init();
886   if (debug_sync_result != 0) {
887     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync system initialization failed");
888     FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Debug sync system initialization failed");
889   }
890   log_debug("Debug sync system initialized successfully");
891 
892   // Initialize memory debug system
893   // TEMPORARILY DISABLED: these calls hang in non-interactive mode
894   // log_debug("Initializing memory debug system...");
895   // int debug_memory_result = debug_memory_thread_init();
896   // if (debug_memory_result != 0) {
897   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug system initialization failed");
898   //   FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Memory debug system initialization failed");
899   // }
900   // log_debug("Memory debug system initialized successfully");
901 
902   // Start memory debug (now just prints report directly)
903   // if (debug_memory_thread_start() != 0) {
904   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug startup failed");
905   //   FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Memory debug startup failed");
906   // }
907 
908 #ifndef _WIN32
909   // Unblock SIGUSR1 and SIGUSR2 at process level to ensure delivery
910   sigset_t set;
911   sigemptyset(&set);
912   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR1);
913   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR2);
914   sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
915 
916   // Register SIGUSR1 to trigger synchronization debugging output in all modes
917   signal_handler_t old_usr1 = platform_signal(SIGUSR1, common_handle_sigusr1);
918   if (old_usr1 == SIG_ERR) {
919     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR1 handler");
920   } else {
921     log_debug("SIGUSR1 handler registered successfully");
922   }
923 
924   // Register SIGUSR2 to trigger memory report in all modes
925   signal_handler_t old_usr2 = platform_signal(SIGUSR2, common_handle_sigusr2);
926   if (old_usr2 == SIG_ERR) {
927     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR2 handler");
928   } else {
929     log_debug("SIGUSR2 handler registered successfully");
930   }
931 #endif
932 #endif
933 
934   if (opts->fps > 0) {
935     if (opts->fps < 1 || opts->fps > 144) {
936       log_warn("FPS value %d out of range (1-144), using default", opts->fps);
937     } else {
938       log_debug("FPS set from command line: %d", opts->fps);
939     }
940   }
941 
942   // Automatic update check at startup (once per week maximum)
943   if (!GET_OPTION(no_check_update)) {
944     update_check_result_t update_result;
945     asciichat_error_t update_err = update_check_startup(&update_result);
946     if (update_err == ASCIICHAT_OK && update_result.update_available) {
947       char notification[1024];
948       update_check_format_notification(&update_result, notification, sizeof(notification));
949       log_info("%s", notification);
950 
951       // Set update notification for splash/status screens
952       splash_set_update_notification(notification);
953     }
954   }
955 
956   // Set up global signal handlers BEFORE mode dispatch
957   // All modes use the same centralized exit mechanism
958   setup_signal_handlers();
959 
960   // Register application callbacks so lib code can check exit flags
961   // This connects the render loop's should_exit check to signal_exit()
962   static const app_callbacks_t app_callbacks = {
963       .should_exit = should_exit,
964       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
965   };
966   app_callbacks_register(&app_callbacks);
967 
968   // Register shutdown callback so splash thread and other code can check for exit
969   shutdown_register_callback((shutdown_check_fn)should_exit);
970 
971 #ifndef NDEBUG
972   // Start debug threads now, after initialization but before mode entry
973   // This avoids lock contention during critical initialization phase
974   // Note: debug_sync_start_thread() and debug_memory_thread_start() now use
975   // direct pthread calls instead of mutex_init/cond_init to avoid named registry
976   // deadlock during thread startup
977   if (debug_sync_start_thread() != 0) {
978     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync thread startup failed");
979     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Debug sync thread startup failed");
980   }
981   log_debug("Debug sync thread started");
982 
983 #endif
984 
985   // Find and dispatch to mode entry point
986   const mode_descriptor_t *mode = find_mode(opts->detected_mode);
987   if (!mode) {
988     log_error("Error: Mode not found for detected_mode=%d", opts->detected_mode);
989     return 1;
990   }
991 
992   // Call the mode-specific entry point
993   // Mode entry points use options_get() to access parsed options
994   int exit_code = mode->entry_point();
995 
996   // Named registry cleanup is handled by asciichat_shared_destroy()
997   // (called via atexit handler), which runs AFTER the memory report
998 
999   if (exit_code == ERROR_USAGE) {
1000     exit(ERROR_USAGE);
1001   }
1002 
1003   return exit_code;
1004 }






References action_show_version(),
    app_callbacks_register(), apply_color_mode_override(),
    apply_palette_config(), argc, argv,
    ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(), asciichat_shared_destroy(),
    asciichat_shared_init(), asciichat_thread_self(),
    backtrace_print_simple(), colorscheme_load_builtin(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    debug_atomic_init(), debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(),
    debug_sync_init(), debug_sync_set_main_thread_id(),
    debug_sync_start_thread(),
    options_state::debug_sync_state_time,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), options_state::detected_mode,
    mode_descriptor_t::entry_point, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, ERROR_THREAD, ERROR_USAGE,
    FATAL, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE, options_state::fps,
    GET_OPTION, grep_init(), HAS_ERRNO,
    options_state::help, IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT, log_debug,
    log_dev, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error, log_file,
    options_state::log_file, log_info, log_init_colors(),
    log_redetect_terminal_capabilities(), log_set_color_scheme(),
    log_set_force_stderr(), log_set_format(),
    log_set_json_output(), log_set_terminal_output(),
    log_truncate_if_large(), log_warn, MODE_CLIENT,
    MODE_DISCOVERY, MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID,
    MODE_MIRROR, MODE_SERVER, named_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD, named_registry_register_packet_types(),
    options_cleanup_schema(), options_colorscheme_init_early(),
    options_get(), options_init(), options_state_destroy(),
    options_state::palette_custom,
    options_state::palette_custom_set,
    path_cleanup_thread_locals(), platform_open(),
    platform_signal(), REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, SET_ERRNO, setup_signal_handlers(),
    should_exit(), shutdown_register_callback(),
    signal_exit(), splash_set_update_notification(),
    options_state::status_screen, terminal_cursor_show(),
    terminal_is_interactive(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    update_check_result_t::update_available,
    update_check_format_notification(), update_check_startup(),
    and options_state::version.






void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 218 of file main.c.


218                                               {
219   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
220 }






Referenced by client_main(), and
  discovery_main().






void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 337 of file main.c.


337                                  {
338   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
339 
340 #ifndef _WIN32
341   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
342       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
343       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
344       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
345   };
346   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
347   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
348 #endif
349 }






References log_debug,
    platform_register_signal_handlers(), and
    platform_set_console_ctrl_handler().


Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 202 of file main.c.


202                        {
203   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
204 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_attempt_websocket(),
    display_full_reset(), main(),
    server_connection_establish(), session_client_like_run(),
    session_pipeline_run_main(), and session_render_loop().






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 206 of file main.c.


206                        {
207   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
208   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
209   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
210   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
211   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
212   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
213   if (cb) {
214     cb();
215   }
216 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.


Referenced by client_main(), main(), and
    session_render_loop().
